Title: RE: email permission
Notes:
Ticket Title: Email Permission Issue – Unable to Send/Receive from Salesforce Address

Summary:
User Sinem Eren (Algro International) reported being unable to send or receive emails from the Salesforce address noreply@salesforce.com and requested permission to allow communication with that account.

Action Taken:

  • Verified the mail logs in Microsoft 365.

  • Found that the address noreply@salesforce.com is a no-reply account, not configured to receive inbound or user-initiated emails.

  • Confirmed the rejection error “550 5.1.1 User Unknown” from Salesforce’s Proofpoint mail gateway, consistent with the behavior of a no-reply address.

  • Informed the user that such addresses can send system-generated messages (e.g., password resets) but cannot receive replies.

  • Additionally, clarified that Microsoft 365 retains quarantined emails for 30 days, and older password reset emails cannot be retrieved once expired.

Resolution:
Explained to the user that the issue is not a permission problem — the Salesforce no-reply address cannot receive or respond to emails by design. User acknowledged the clarification.

Status: Closed – Issue resolved through clarification; no mail configuration changes required.

Title: RE: Technician - INSTALL issues with FoxIt Editor
Notes:
Ticket Summary:
User Patricia Quezada reported that she was unable to complete the installation or upgrade of Foxit PDF Editor, as it was requesting admin credentials.

Action Taken:

  • Connected to the user’s system and found that the upgrade to Foxit PDF Editor V14 was failing during installation.

  • Removed the existing license and retried installation — still failed.

  • Performed a full cleanup, rebooted, and attempted again — issue persisted.

  • Repaired the existing installation — failed again.

  • Uninstalled the application completely, rebooted the system, and reinstalled the correct version (V13) from the admin portal.

  • Verified documentation confirming that the current license only supports V13, not V14.

  • Reinstalled V13, verified the installation, and confirmed with the user that all PDF editor functions were working properly.

Status: Completed and Verified

Title: Deepak - Computer Issue
Notes: We connected to the workstation and performed a full performance review. Multiple Autodesk background processes were found running, which were contributing to high resource usage. We reviewed the Task Manager, terminated unnecessary Autodesk services, and checked the system for DistributedCOM errors in the Event Viewer. We also analyzed system CPU, RAM, and disk performance to identify bottlenecks. Based on the findings, we confirmed that system slowness is related to heavy background processes, disabled the apps that are not required for auto startup. Cleanup done in machine and increased page size. After that reboot the machine, now the machine is working much better, as confirmed by Deepak.

Title: Informational-severity alert: Creation of forwarding/redirect rule
Notes:
The informational alert for MailRedirect activity was triggered for the user najmeh@algrointernational.com This alert indicates that a forwarding or redirect rule was created for the user's email,

Reviewed the Exchange Admin Center and found no active forwarding or redirect rules for the user. Additionally, we checked the mail flow rules but did not identify any suspicious configurations.

I also reviewed the user's mailbox settings for any visible forwarding rules but found nothing out of the ordinary. The alert was likely triggered by a change to the user's mailbox settings, which may have been authorized by the user or an admin.

I also reviewed the alert details and activity logs. The screenshot showed that the MailRedirect activity was recorded and linked to the creation of the forwarding rule. However, no further details were found within the logs that suggest unauthorized activity.

Title: Veeam O365 configurations for ALG
Notes: Performed a complete end-to-end setup of the Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 environment for the ALG client, beginning with logging into the QNAP NAS and configuring a dedicated iSCSI target and LUN on the isolated backup network to ensure optimized throughput and secure storage segmentation. After creating the iSCSI storage, logged into the Veeam 365 backup server and used the Windows iSCSI Initiator to connect to the newly created QNAP target, ensuring persistent binding for automatic reconnection. Once connected, initialized the disk in Disk Management, formatted it with NTFS, assigned an appropriate volume label, and verified that the storage was fully accessible for use as a Veeam repository. Proceeded to the Microsoft Azure portal under the ALG tenant and created the required Azure Application Registration for Veeam, configuring all necessary modern app-only authentication settings, generating and securely recording the client secret, and assigning the correct API permissions and Azure roles needed for Veeam to access Microsoft 365 resources. After confirming the application setup, returned to the Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 console and added the ALG Microsoft 365 organization using the newly created Azure app credentials, validating successful communication with Exchange Online, SharePoint Online, OneDrive for Business, and Microsoft Teams workloads. Then created a new backup repository within the Veeam V365 environment and mapped it to the previously attached iSCSI volume, confirming retention settings and repository health. With storage and authentication correctly configured, created the ALG backup job, selected all required services, applied the proper job settings, and initiated the first backup run. Verified that the job started successfully, monitored initial throughput and logging, and confirmed that the backup job is currently running and progressing as expected without errors.

Ticket Summary for Patricia's Laptop (ALG-HQ-LPT-03)

Issue Summary:

  • Customer: Patricia
  • Device: ALG-HQ-LPT-03
  • Issue: Slowness and system performance issues.
  • Reported Problem: Patricia’s laptop was experiencing significant slowness and was not responsive.

Troubleshooting Steps Taken:

  1. Initial Diagnosis:
    • Ran hardware diagnostics to rule out hardware issues — no significant issues were detected.
    • Performed a software update, but the system remained slow.
  2. Further Troubleshooting:
    • Verified system settings and ran SFC (System File Checker) scan to fix potential system file corruption.
    • Despite multiple troubleshooting efforts, the system continued to be slow, and the resource utilization remained high.
  3. Conclusion:
    • Based on system performance metrics, it was determined that the issue could not be resolved with standard troubleshooting and required a reinstallation (ISO).
    • Backup: All necessary files were backed up to OneDrive for data safety.

Next Steps:

    • Patricia's laptop was replaced.
  2. Next Actions:
    • New laptop setup and configuration ticket will be created for installing and setting up the new device.

Ticket Closure:

  • This ticket has been closed as Patricia’s laptop was replaced and all troubleshooting was completed.

Title: Laptop cleanup and One drive
Notes:
Diana requested that the laptop be wiped and cleaned. Before proceeding, I reached out to the user to confirm everything was in order. The user reported that certain folders appeared to be missing. After remotely connecting to the device, I verified that the folders were present.

Later, the user reported that files within those folders were missing. As a result, the backup did not complete properly, which caused some files not to transfer.

We waited for the user to review and confirm that all required files were successfully received on the new laptop. Once the user confirmed, we created a full backup of the remaining data, wiped the old laptop, and reinstalled Windows.

Title: One Drive Issue
Notes:
Received a mail from Shahrokh regarding his inability to log into OneDrive. After reviewing the issue, I proceeded with resetting the password via Office 365 and provided the new login credentials to him. Shahrokh then logged into OneDrive both on the Chrome browser and desktop app.

Despite logging in successfully, OneDrive encountered a syncing issue. I advised Shahrokh to sign out or unlink OneDrive and then sign back in with the new credentials. After doing this, OneDrive entered syncing mode, and I informed him that it takes some time to complete.

I then accessed his computer remotely via SSH to verify the issue. After confirming that OneDrive was now syncing correctly, I updated Shahrokh on the situation, ensuring he was satisfied with the solution.

Once the issue was resolved and Shahrokh confirmed everything was working as expected, I closed the ticket. A confirmation email was sent to the user.

Ticket successfully closed after confirmation from Shahrokh.

Title: Jyoti with ALG need assistance with her computer screen going black with lines across it and the slowness of her system
Notes: To resolve the slowness issue on the machine DESKTOP-8S6IF2G, we identified resource-heavy processes using Task Manager, such as Google Chrome and Outlook, and closed unnecessary applications. Adjusted the power settings to High Performance for optimal operation, and cleaned up temporary files and browser cache. A full antivirus scan was run to ensure there were no malware threats, and system drivers were updated for improved performance. We also disabled unnecessary startup programs, reviewed resource exhaustion logs, and restarted the system to apply the changes. After reboot asked user to check and confirm the performance, waiting for user response.
